description:
You are a deployment engineer preparing tonight’s roll-out.  
    A colleague has already copied every package that still needs to be pushed into  

        /home/user/deployment/pending_updates

    All of those files have the extension “.rpm”.  
    Your job is to create, with ONE shell command, a short log file that states how many RPM
    packages are currently waiting there.

    1. Use any single, ordinary shell pipeline you like (no interactive editors, no temporary
       helper scripts) to generate the file  

           /home/user/deployment/update_summary.log

    2. The file must contain **exactly one line** (followed by a single newline) using the
       precise format shown below—no extra spaces, no additional lines:

           Pending RPM packages: <COUNT>

       where <COUNT> is the integer number of “*.rpm” files that exist inside
       /home/user/deployment/pending_updates at the moment your command runs.

    3. If /home/user/deployment/update_summary.log already exists, your command must overwrite
       it rather than appending to it.

    4. After running your command, you can check your work by displaying the file’s
       contents in the terminal, but that is optional.  The automated test will validate:

       • The file /home/user/deployment/update_summary.log exists.  
       • The file contains exactly one line in the format described above, followed by a
         terminating newline character.  
       • <COUNT> correctly reflects the actual number of “*.rpm” files presently located in
         /home/user/deployment/pending_updates.

    Tips (not requirements):
       • Tools such as ls, find, wc, or printf can be combined in a pipeline.  
       • Remember to redirect the final output into the target log file (using “>”, NOT “>>”).  
       • Try to keep everything on a single command line.

    Once you are confident, run your one-liner.  Nothing else needs to be modified.
truth:
Initial directory state BEFORE the agent starts:

      /home/user/deployment/pending_updates/
          ├── app-core-2.0.1.rpm
          ├── db-driver-5.3.0.rpm
          └── ui-frontend-1.4.8.rpm

    Therefore the correct count is 3.

    After successful task completion, the file
      /home/user/deployment/update_summary.log
    must exist with exact byte contents (including the trailing newline):

      "Pending RPM packages: 3\n"
name: task_000000_57d821bb
